description_long: This object is a quirky, retro-styled electronic device featuring a textured, boxy body with a woven orange front panel and four short feet. The front face displays a large, protruding lens or eye-like component on the left, a horizontal slot, and a set of wooden, unevenly spaced keys or levers below. Atop the device sits a blue-striped pyramid supporting a double metal ring antenna, with a thick red cable looping from the back to the top. The right side of the top panel is equipped with four toggle switches and a blue button, while the left side features a large red push button. The materials appear to be a mix of metal, plastic, wood, and possibly rubber for the cable. The overall design suggests a whimsical, possibly custom-built electronic gadget, perhaps for experimental or artistic purposes.
description: A retro, boxy electronic device with a woven orange front, a large lens, wooden keys, a pyramid antenna, and multiple switches and buttons.
description_view_0: The front view shows the orange woven panel, large lens, wooden keys, horizontal slot, and the pyramid antenna with metal rings on top.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the large red push button and the protruding lens, with the pyramid antenna and red cable visible on top.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the smooth black back, the red cable looping across the top, and the pyramid antenna with metal rings.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the four toggle switches, blue button, and the side of the wooden keys, with the red cable and pyramid antenna visible.
attribute_control_interface: physical buttons, toggle switches, wooden keys
